menu.whatToDo: What do you want to do?

run.dryRunNote: Dry run: showing workflow structure without executing.
run.noWorkflows: No workflows found. Run: eddgate init

panel.pressEnterRun: Press Enter to configure and run.
panel.rulesAutoLoaded: Generated rules auto-loaded on next run

analyzeOutput.title: eddgate analyze
analyzeOutput.noTraces: No traces found. Run a workflow first.
analyzeOutput.fixScoreClose: Scores close to threshold. Options: (1) lower threshold, (2) improve prompt, (3) add few-shot examples
analyzeOutput.fixScoreModerate: Moderate eval failures. Check: (1) retrieval relevance, (2) prompt clarity, (3) split into smaller steps
analyzeOutput.fixScoreSevere: Severe eval failures. Step may need fundamental redesign
analyzeOutput.fixTooShort: Output too short. Add detail requirements or lower min length
analyzeOutput.fixMissingFields: Missing required fields. Add output format example to prompt
analyzeOutput.fixValidation: Validation mismatch. Rules may be too strict for model output
analyzeOutput.fixRateLimit: Rate limits wasting tokens. Add delays, use smaller model, reduce retries
analyzeOutput.fixTimeout: Timeouts. Check connection or increase timeout

run.title: Run -- Workflow Execution
run.desc: Pick a YAML file from templates/workflows/ and run it. Example: select document-pipeline.yaml and it will classify -> retrieve -> write -> verify automatically.
run.how1: 1. A workflow list appears -> use arrows to pick one
run.how2: 2. Click 'Select file' to open a file browser -> pick your .txt, .md, .pdf, etc.
run.how3: 3. Choose a model (Sonnet is fine for most tasks, Opus for complex ones)
run.how4: 4. Choose 'Start run' -> left panel shows step progress, right panel shows live logs
run.gate2: Tier 2: AI scores output accuracy (only at critical steps)
run.optReport: Report -> Creates report.html when done. Open in browser to see pretty results
run.optTrace: Trace -> Saves a .jsonl file in traces/. You analyze this file in the 'Analyze' menu later
run.optBudget: Budget -> Type 0.5 to auto-stop if cost exceeds 50 cents
run.optDry: Dry-run -> Shows workflow structure without calling AI. Try this first when learning!
run.when1: Ex: Feed a paper.pdf into document-pipeline to get a summary + analysis report
run.when2: Ex: Select bug-fix.yaml and paste an error log -> auto root-cause + fix + verify
run.when3: Ex: Use translation.yaml to translate English docs to Korean + accuracy check
run.when4: New here? Start with Dry-run to see the structure without spending anything

analyze.title: Analyze -- Failure Analysis
analyze.desc: Reads .jsonl files from traces/ folder. You need to enable trace saving in the Run menu first so there are files to analyze.
analyze.subTitle: What can I do here?
analyze.sub1: Failure Analysis: reads traces/*.jsonl to find 'which step failed, why, how many times'. Also auto-generates fix rules.
analyze.sub2: Context Profiler: finds if AI is wasting tokens by reprocessing the same content repeatedly.
analyze.sub3: A/B Test: pick 2 prompt files from templates/prompts/, run the same task, and statistically compare which is better.
analyze.sub4: Diff Eval: compare quality scores between git commit A and commit B.
analyze.sub5: Version Diff: shows what changed in your prompt/workflow YAML files recently.
analyze.loopNote: Generated rules are saved to eval/rules/ and auto-loaded on the next Run.
analyze.when1: Run produced bad results -> pick 'Failure Analysis' -> it reads traces/ automatically
analyze.when2: You edited a prompt but not sure if it helped -> 'A/B Test' compares old vs new
analyze.when3: Costs seem too high -> 'Context Profiler' shows where tokens are wasted
analyze.when4: Note: if traces/ folder is empty, there's nothing to analyze. Enable trace in Run first

test.title: Test -- Regression Testing
test.desc: Save a copy of today's good results (snapshot), then after making changes, auto-check that nothing got worse.
test.subTitle: Do it in order
test.sub1: 1. First 'Snapshot' -- saves current results from traces/ into .snapshots/ as your baseline
test.sub2: 2. Make your changes (edit prompts, switch models, etc.) and run again
test.sub3: 3. Then 'Diff' -- compares new results vs saved baseline. Red = worse, Green = better
test.ciNote: For GitHub Actions: add 'eddgate test diff --ci' to auto-block PRs that lower quality.
test.when1: Pipeline works well -> 'Snapshot' to save this good state for reference
test.when2: You edited a prompt -> 'Diff' to check nothing regressed
test.when3: Switched from Sonnet to Haiku -> 'Diff' to see quality difference visually
test.when4: Note: You need a snapshot first before you can Diff. Create snapshot first

monitor.title: Monitor -- Metrics Dashboard
monitor.desc: Reads all .jsonl files from traces/ and shows aggregate statistics in charts and tables.
monitor.dash1: Status: shows success rate as a bar chart, which steps fail most often
monitor.dash2: Cost: table showing spend by model (Sonnet/Opus) and by step. See where money goes
monitor.dash3: Quality: eval scores per step as avg/min/max. Green if > 0.7, red if < 0.4
monitor.dataNote: More .jsonl files in traces/ = more accurate statistics.
monitor.when1: You've run workflows several times and want to see the overall picture
monitor.when2: Want to check this month's AI spending -> 'Cost' dashboard
monitor.when3: Want to find the weakest step -> 'Quality' dashboard, look for red items
monitor.when4: Note: if traces/ is empty, nothing will show. Run some workflows first

traces.title: Traces -- Event Timeline
traces.desc: Pick a specific .jsonl file from traces/ and see a detailed timeline of what happened: when each step ran, what AI produced, and whether checks passed.
traces.feat1: Left panel: step list (classify, retrieve, generate...) with status and duration
traces.feat3: Failed validation steps show red, low eval scores show yellow
traces.feat4: Select an error step to see the full error message
traces.navNote: Press Tab to switch focus between steps (left) and timeline (right).
traces.when1: A run failed and you want to see exactly which step and why
traces.when2: Step 3 output looked weird -> check what AI actually produced at that step
traces.when3: Run was expensive -> see which step used the most tokens
traces.when4: You'll pick a .jsonl file first. No files? Enable trace saving in Run menu

mcp.title: MCP -- Server Management
mcp.desc: If your workflow YAML uses tools like mcp:pinecone:search-records, the MCP server must be connected first. Manage server connections here.
mcp.act1: List: shows all connected MCP servers and their available tools
mcp.act2: Add: connect a new server. You'll enter a name, transport type (stdio/http), and command or URL
mcp.act3: Remove: pick a server from the list and disconnect it
mcp.intNote: Tools format in workflow YAML: mcp:<server-name>:<tool-name>
mcp.claudeNote: MCP servers already configured in Claude Code are detected automatically.
mcp.when1: Want to use rag-pipeline.yaml but need Pinecone server -> 'Add' to connect it
mcp.when2: Got 'tool not found' error during a run -> 'List' to check if the server is connected
mcp.when3: Cleaning up servers you no longer use -> 'Remove'

plugins.title: Plugins -- Extensions & Tools
plugins.desc: Manage files in templates/ folder: workflows, roles, plus developer tools like RAG indexing, visualization, and debugging.
plugins.feat1: Workflow list: shows .yaml files in templates/workflows/
plugins.feat2: Role list: shows agent role definitions in templates/roles/
plugins.feat3: Import: copy a .yaml file from elsewhere into templates/
plugins.feat4: RAG indexing: load .txt/.md files into a vector DB so AI can search them
plugins.feat5: Visualize: draw a diagram of your workflow (shows step connections at a glance)
plugins.feat6: Step debug: run just one step from a workflow to test it in isolation
plugins.feat7: Deploy gate: check all quality thresholds pass in one go (for CI)
plugins.when1: A colleague sent you a workflow .yaml -> 'Import' to add it to templates/
plugins.when2: Step 3 is buggy -> 'Step debug' to run only that step and inspect output
plugins.when3: You have 20 PDFs to make searchable by AI -> 'RAG indexing' to load into vector DB
plugins.when4: Workflow is complex and confusing -> 'Visualize' to see the diagram

config.title: Config -- Settings
config.desc: Modify eddgate.config.yaml settings. No need to edit the file manually -- change everything from here.
config.set2: Language: switches all text in this UI between English and Korean
config.set3: Doctor: checks Node.js version, Claude CLI install, config validity, workflow graphs -- all at once
config.set4: Init: creates templates/, traces/, and basic config in an empty folder automatically
config.when1: Just installed -> 'Init' to create project structure -> 'Doctor' to verify everything works
config.when2: Want to save money -> change default model from Opus to Sonnet or Haiku
config.when3: Suddenly getting errors -> 'Doctor' checks Node.js, Claude CLI, config files all at once
config.when4: Prefer Korean UI -> 'Language' to switch (applies immediately)
